title: Tell me when to stop
summary: An old barber and his longest customer. The customer cannot see the mirror anymore. He still comes every month.

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: Good morning, Bác Tâm. Same chair?

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: Same chair. Same haircut.

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: Sit down. How are you today?

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: My eyes are worse. I cannot see the mirror anymore.

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: Then I will tell you what I see.

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: Thank you. Tell me when to stop talking, too.

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: You never talk too much. Now — short on the sides?

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: Short on the sides. A little longer on top.

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: Like always.

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: How many years have I come here?

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: Thirty-one.

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: Thirty-one years. And you remember my haircut?

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: I remember everything.

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: Mr Hai, may I ask a small question?

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: Please.

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: Do I look the same?

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: Your hair is white now. It was black when you first came. Your face is the same. Your eyes are kind. Your smile is the same too.

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: And the mirror?

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: The mirror shows a man with white hair, a kind face, and a good shirt today.

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: Good. Thank you.

body.speaker: Mr Hai
body.text: Done. Tell me when to stop.

body.speaker: Bác Tâm
body.text: Stop. It is perfect.

annotations.meaningVi: tệ hơn / yếu hơn (so sánh hơn của 'bad')
nữa (trong câu phủ định: không ... nữa)
cắt ngắn hai bên (thuật ngữ tiệm tóc)
để dài hơn một chút ở trên đầu
như mọi khi / như thường lệ
cho phép tôi hỏi (câu hỏi lịch sự, trang trọng hơn 'can I ask')
lần đầu tiên bạn đến (mệnh đề thời gian với 'when')
một cái áo đẹp ('good' dùng thay 'nice' để khen trang phục)

grammarNotes.title: Imperative and 'Tell me' requests
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu mệnh lệnh dùng động từ nguyên thể ở đầu câu để yêu cầu lịch sự một việc làm. 'Tell me when to stop.' = 'Hãy nói cho tôi biết khi nào dừng.' 'Sit down.' = 'Ngồi xuống.' Câu hỏi lịch sự 'May I ask...?' = 'Cho phép tôi hỏi...?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: An imperative starts with the base form of the verb. 'Sit down.' 'Tell me when to stop.' Add 'please' to make it gentler, or use 'May I…?' for a polite question: 'May I ask a small question?'

translationVi:
Ông Hai: Chào bác Tâm. Vẫn ghế cũ nhé?
Bác Tâm: Vẫn ghế cũ. Vẫn kiểu tóc cũ.
Ông Hai: Mời ngồi. Hôm nay bác khỏe không?
Bác Tâm: Mắt tôi yếu hơn. Tôi không nhìn thấy gương nữa.
Ông Hai: Vậy để tôi tả lại cho bác xem.
Bác Tâm: Cảm ơn. Khi nào tôi nói nhiều quá, ông cũng bảo tôi dừng nhé.
Ông Hai: Bác có nói nhiều bao giờ đâu. Nào — cắt ngắn hai bên?
Bác Tâm: Ngắn hai bên. Trên đầu để dài hơn một chút.
Ông Hai: Như mọi khi.
Bác Tâm: Tôi đến đây bao nhiêu năm rồi nhỉ?
Ông Hai: Ba mươi mốt năm.
Bác Tâm: Ba mươi mốt năm. Mà ông nhớ kiểu tóc của tôi?
Ông Hai: Tôi nhớ tất cả.
Bác Tâm: Ông Hai ơi, cho tôi hỏi một câu nhỏ nhé.
Ông Hai: Bác cứ hỏi.
Bác Tâm: Tôi có còn giống như trước không?
Ông Hai: Tóc bác giờ bạc rồi. Lần đầu bác đến tóc còn đen. Khuôn mặt vẫn vậy. Đôi mắt hiền. Nụ cười cũng vậy.
Bác Tâm: Còn trong gương thì sao?
Ông Hai: Trong gương là một người đàn ông tóc bạc, khuôn mặt hiền, hôm nay mặc cái áo đẹp.
Bác Tâm: Tốt. Cảm ơn ông.
Ông Hai: Xong rồi. Bác bảo tôi dừng khi nào nhé.
Bác Tâm: Dừng. Hoàn hảo lắm.

questions.explanation: Mr Hai is the barber; Bác Tâm is the customer.
questions.hint: Look at who says 'Sit down. How are you today?'

questions.prompt: Why can't Bác Tâm see the mirror anymore?
options: Mr Hai took the mirror away.
questions.explanation: 'My eyes are worse. I cannot see the mirror anymore.'

questions.prompt: What does Mr Hai offer to do for Bác Tâm?
options: To tell him what he sees.
To give him a different haircut.
questions.explanation: 'Then I will tell you what I see.'
questions.hint: Mr Hai's reply about the mirror.

questions.prompt: What haircut does Bác Tâm want?
options: Short on the sides, a little longer on top
questions.explanation: 'Short on the sides. A little longer on top.'
questions.hint: Bác Tâm describes his usual cut.

questions.prompt: How many years has Bác Tâm been coming to this barber?
questions.hint: Mr Hai gives the exact number.

questions.prompt: What was Bác Tâm's hair colour before?
questions.explanation: 'Your hair is white now. It was black.'
questions.hint: Mr Hai compares now and before.

questions.prompt: What does Mr Hai say about Bác Tâm's eyes?
What does 'Tell me when to stop' mean at the end?
options: 'Tell me when the haircut is finished enough.'
'Tell me when to close the shop.'
questions.explanation: Bác Tâm cannot see the mirror, so Mr Hai asks him to say when the cut is right.
questions.hint: Mr Hai says it just before Bác Tâm says 'Stop. It is perfect.'

questions.prompt: How do the two men feel about each other?
options: They are close — a long friendship inside small monthly visits.
questions.explanation: Thirty-one years of haircuts, and Mr Hai 'remembers everything.'
questions.hint: Look at how long Bác Tâm has been coming.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the conversation?
options: Long, small habits can hold a quiet kind of care.
Old people should not cut their hair.
Barbers should not talk to customers.
questions.explanation: A monthly haircut and a careful description of a face become a kind of friendship.
questions.hint: Think about the long routine they share.

Mr Hai: The mirror shows a man with white hair, a kind face, and a good {{b9}} today.

blanks.explanation: Bác Tâm always sits in the same chair.
He can no longer see the mirror.
Mr Hai will tell him what he sees.
Short on the sides — Bác Tâm's usual cut.
Mr Hai remembers everything about him.
His hair is white now (it was black).
He is wearing a good shirt today.
Bác Tâm says it is perfect.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Hai becomes Bác Tâm's eyes — describing the mirror he can no longer see. Is there a small task someone has quietly done for you over many years that you only noticed recently? What did you do, or could you do, when you noticed?
